narrative.summary: NEW Michelin 1-star (Oct 2025). Chef Pete Ho's intimate yakitori bar. Charcoal-grilled Japanese perfection. Opened early 2025 and already starred — that's the real signal. Mount Pleasant location. Worth a deliberate stop for John’s design/architecture/art/food radar.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're on Main Street in Mount Pleasant, pushing past the breweries and pho shops to a narrow yakitori counter where eight seats face Chef Pete Ho and his binchōtan charcoal grill. The room is stripped back—pale wood, focused task lighting, smoke curling up toward the ventilation hood. It's designed to make you watch: the grill, the hands, the char forming on each skewer.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: This is omakase yakitori, which means you're here for whatever Ho pulls from the charcoal that night—chicken heart with its dense chew, gloriously fatty chicken artery, a thick-cut thigh steak that eats like beef. The menu moves through the whole bird, each skewer timed to the second, plus an oyster add-on that somehow makes sense, hand-pulled noodles to close, and a broth you'll want to drink from the bowl. Expect to spend around $100-120 for the full procession.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: Book this for a solo seat at the counter when you want to be fully locked in—no conversation competing with the choreography of the grill. Or bring one other person who understands that dinner here is participatory, not background.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Long live Sumibiyaki Arashi. Chef Pete Ho is a gangster. He is an elite athlete. He is a force. This was one of the best dining experiences I've ever had. There's a card when you sit down for your omakase that encourages you to enjoy the experience with all five senses. And you have to. From the calming aesthetic and ambiance, to the charcoal smells, to the focused faces, to vibing music, to the mouth watering first bite and to the last - its all just an experience you have to be present for in and take in.

My standouts were the oyster add-on, the chicken steak, the noodles, chicken heart, the artery, the wings, the broth and every other dish put in front of me.

It was over two years ago that another chef recommended I look out for Sumibiyaki Arashi. I got to try Pete's popups while he and his team diligently built the restaurant, and navigated having a charcoal grill up front and center for patrons to see. It was worth two years of waiting for it to open its doors. It was worth two months of waiting for my reservation date. It was worth the two (and a bit) hours of sitting in the restaurant. Book when you can, whenever you can - you will want to experience this place at least once in your life. Long live Sumibiyaki Arashi."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "It was a Seventeen items course.

Menu items does change based on season's best produce. At the time we visit (Mid October), we had

Sea urchin on pan seared scallop + pickled cherry tomato with arugula
Robata Skewered {Chicken tender, thigh, heart}
Wagyu slice on rice with shishito pepper, coddled quail egg and fine minced chive
Skewered drumstick
Pickeled Apple and ginger crisp
Tori Paitan (1st extract)
Fried tofu skewer with micro sliced scallion, arugula and roasted shizo and sesame
Shishito pepper
Chawanmushi with red crab and yuzu peel
Chicken knee
Chicken wing de-boned
Tsukune with egg drizzle
Onsen Tamago on minced chicken rice
Chicken soup (2nd extract)
Dessert (Pear --> Muscat Grape --> Yaki Imo)

All prepared to perfection.
Over the course of 2 hours and 10 minutes, everything was grilled and plated in front of you.
Serves slow, good stuff takes time.
But in the end you'll be full for sure, cause it's a lot of food.

On a scale of 1 to 10
1 being under salted
5 being perfect
10 being over salted

Chef is able to constantly land on a 5 to 6 - Right on the dot or marginally over salt a little
Salt brings out the freshness of the ingredient, all packed with flavor, done with precision, nothing over salted.
And I really like the charcoal fragrant on chicken!

Good luck getting a reservation though!
Initially I was planning to visit in JULY for my wife's BDay. Missed the chance 3 times and had to come mid week October. That's how hard it is to reserve.
Staff was caring and friendly. Try your very best to reserve on your special occasion's month. We only had about 3 ~ 5 minutes to make a reservation at 9am.
But it's definitely worth every penny of that $175 per person."

⭐⭐⭐⭐ "3.5 stars. Everything was prepared excellently, but after a while, my friend and I tired of the chicken and charcoal taste. I wish there was a little more variety in terms of flavour, maybe even just a different sauce for some of the chicken skewers. Ideally there would be more fish, beef, or vegetable bites throughout the meal to break up the monotony of the chicken skewers. Wish i could give 3.5 stars as I only give 4 or 5 stars to places I would return to, but I rounded up because I recognize the quality of their food."
